briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority approves 2026–2030 Strategic Plan, shaping city housing development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) Corporation's five-year strategic plan for 2026–2030. This plan will guide how the BHA develops and manages affordable housing in Burnaby over the next five years, which may affect local construction, development, and property management activity.

briefs.title: BC municipality approves $36,250.57 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: A BC municipal council has approved paying $36,250.57 (including taxes) in membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) — the provincial association that represents local governments — for the 2026 year. This is a routine government expense with no direct impact on local businesses.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: A municipal council has awarded a $4.2 million contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water metering technology. This is a public procurement (government purchasing) decision and does not directly change rules or regulations for private businesses.

briefs.title: Modular four-plex pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue sent back for further review
briefs.summary: City council has sent the contract award for a modular four-plex (a small apartment building with four units built off-site in sections) pilot project back to staff. The review is meant to find ways to manage the project more efficiently before moving forward.

briefs.title: 19 contractors awarded hired equipment services contracts paid by actual quantities delivered
briefs.summary: A local council approved contracts with 19 separate contractors to provide hired equipment services. Payments to these contractors will be based on the actual amount of work or materials delivered, not a fixed price agreed in advance.

briefs.title: $1.155M asphalt paving contract renewed with Blackrete Paving by municipal council
briefs.summary: A local municipal council has renewed its asphalt paving services contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for an estimated $1,155,000 including taxes. This is a government procurement decision — meaning the contract to supply paving services has been awarded to an existing vendor and is not open for new bids at this time.

briefs.title: Mixed-use high-density development rezoning accepted for Carrigan Court properties
briefs.summary: The City has accepted a rezoning application for two properties at 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court. Staff will now work with the applicant to develop a detailed plan for a high-density building that mixes commercial and residential units — both strata (individually owned) and rental — before bringing it back to Council for a decision.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Council agendas for the public hearing on this rezoning if your business operates nearby.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Grand Promenade towers rezoned: 362 new strata units added, office space replaced with hotel
briefs.summary: Council has approved steps to rezone the towers at 3900 Grand Promenade. The plan removes commercial office space and replaces it with a hotel and restaurant. It also adds 362 strata units (privately owned condos) and updates the Housing Agreement for the nearby Carrigan site.
briefs.action_required: If your business leases or plans to lease office space at 3900 Grand Promenade, contact the developer now to clarify your tenancy status.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: 12-Storey, 290-Unit Mixed-Use Building at 6535 Kingsway Moves Closer to Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (a formal rule change that allows a different type of building on a property) for 6535 Kingsway. If approved, the bylaw would allow a 12-storey building with 290 homes and ground-floor commercial space on that site.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby rezoning bylaw progress if your business operates near 6535 Kingsway.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Five-Storey Self-Storage Facility Rezoning at 2757 Norland Avenue Moves Forward
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw (a rule that changes how a piece of land can be used) for 2757 Norland Avenue to allow a five-storey self-storage building. No public hearing is required because the proposal already lines up with the city's Official Community Plan (the long-term land use blueprint for the city).

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Poverty Reduction Strategy and seeks provincial funding
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially endorsed a Poverty Reduction Strategy and directed staff to build an implementation plan alongside community partners. The Mayor has also been asked to write to the provincial government requesting funding to support poverty reduction efforts in the city.

briefs.title: City Council receives 2025 investment year-end report and 2026 forecast — no action required
briefs.summary: City Council has received and reviewed the 2025 Investment Program year-end report along with the 2026 investment forecast, both prepared by the Financial Management Committee. This was an information item only — no new rules, fees, or requirements were introduced for businesses.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan project officially initiated
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has received and acknowledged the project initiation for the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan. An OCP is a long-term blueprint that guides how land is used, developed, and grown across the city — this action plan will map out the concrete steps to put that vision into practice.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 5058 Imperial Street rezoning in Burnaby advances after first and second reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council gave first and second reading — the first two formal approval steps — to a zoning amendment (a change to the rules about how a specific property can be used or developed) for 5058 Imperial Street. The vote passed despite opposition from four council members, meaning the bylaw moves closer to final approval.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor future council readings and the final vote.

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 6800 Lougheed Highway — what nearby property owners need to know
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to the rules about how a specific piece of land can be used) for the property at 6800 Lougheed Highway. This moves the rezoning process forward, with further readings and approval still required before the change becomes final.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council agendas for the public hearing date to submit comments or attend if your business is affected.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue — first and second reading passed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the first and second reading of a zoning amendment (a change to the rules about how land can be used or developed) for two properties at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. This moves the rezoning application forward in the approval process, but a final decision has not yet been made.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property near 2209–2225 Springer Avenue, monitor future council readings and the public hearing date.

briefs.title: Burnaby updates rules for parking meters and EV charging meters
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed an amendment (a formal change) to its Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw 2019. This means the rules governing how parking meters and EV charging stations operate in Burnaby have been updated.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates parking or EV charging meters in Burnaby and review the updated bylaw for compliance.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Burnaby updates its official fee schedule — some business costs may change
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment No. 2, 2026 to its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (the city's master list of fees for permits, licences, and services). This means certain fees the city charges businesses and residents have been updated and are now in effect.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to see if any permits or licences you pay for have changed in cost.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Rules Updated — What Local Business Owners Need to Know
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment No. 2, 2026 to its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This bylaw sets out how the City issues and enforces bylaw tickets (official written notices that a rule has been broken and a fine may apply). The specific changes made by this amendment are not detailed in the source, so owners should check with the City of Burnaby directly to understand what has changed.
briefs.action_required: Check with the City of Burnaby to find out exactly what changed in the updated enforcement bylaw.

briefs.title: 190 Residents Oppose Rezoning of 5058 Imperial Street — Petition Received by Council
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has received a petition signed by 190 residents opposing the rezoning (a request to change how a property can legally be used) of 5058 Imperial Street. The petition was submitted by neighbours from 5051 Imperial Street and the surrounding area, and Council has acknowledged it for information.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor Council meetings for rezoning decision updates.
